label: Northern front and Lebanon campaigns
scope: Northern border and Lebanon-focused campaigns that best match the game's existing northern-front map framing and escalation model.

operations.name: Operation Litani
operations.theater: South Lebanon up to the Litani River
operations.summary: A cross-border operation in south Lebanon launched after a deadly coastal attack, intended to push hostile armed groups away from the Israeli border.
historicalDynamics: northern villages were vulnerable to infiltration and shelling
buffer depth was treated as a security objective
short campaigns could still create long-term border commitments

candidateObjectives: reduce infiltration pressure on northern towns
build a security buffer without stalling into attrition
hold border communities while pushing hostile launch sites back
mechanicIdeas: border raid events that bypass front lines unless buffer sectors are held
security-zone control that reduces future spawn frequency
civilian evacuation meter tied to northern morale

operations.name: Operation Peace for Galilee
operations.theater: Lebanon from the border to Beirut
operations.summary: A large-scale Lebanon campaign that began as a northern security operation and expanded into a broader and more politically fraught war.
historicalDynamics: mission expansion changed the scale and cost of the conflict
urban fighting created a slower and more punishing tempo
initial border-security logic did not capture the full campaign burden

candidateObjectives: decide whether to stop at a limited objective or push deeper
manage rising costs as urban warfare expands
protect northern cities while operating far from home bases
mechanicIdeas: optional objectives that increase reward but raise escalation
occupation fatigue reducing income over time
mission-branching between limited and expanded end states

operations.name: Operation Accountability
operations.summary: A week-long campaign aimed at suppressing Hezbollah attacks and restoring deterrence on the northern border.
historicalDynamics: rocket and artillery pressure created short warning cycles
political goals centered on deterrence more than occupation
civilian displacement shaped both sides of the campaign

candidateObjectives: limit rocket damage to northern population centers
build deterrence score without triggering unsustainable escalation
keep shelters and emergency services effective under repeated attacks
mechanicIdeas: deterrence score that modifies next-wave intensity
civil-defense purchases that specifically protect northern cities
rocket launch cells that relocate unless pinned quickly

operations.name: Operation Grapes of Wrath
operations.summary: A sustained northern campaign against Hezbollah marked by heavy bombardment, large-scale civilian disruption, and a ceasefire framework focused on limiting attacks on civilians.
historicalDynamics: northern civilian pressure was central to strategic decision-making
the campaign mixed military pressure with political signaling
rapid tactical actions had outsized strategic consequences

candidateObjectives: protect northern towns through a long barrage cycle
balance military retaliation with restraint thresholds
sustain economy and morale while waves remain unpredictable
mechanicIdeas: escalation meter that punishes overuse of the strongest response tools
ceasefire threshold that can end the level early if conditions hold
northern economy penalties that stack with every successful rocket hit

operations.name: Second Lebanon War
operations.summary: A month-long war on the northern front marked by mass rocket fire into Israel, air operations, and contested ground actions in Lebanon.
historicalDynamics: large rocket salvos stressed civilian resilience and interception capacity
air power alone could not fully remove dispersed launch threats
northern daily life became part of the strategic battlespace

candidateObjectives: maintain northern city functionality during prolonged rocket fire
use layered defenses to absorb varied salvo sizes
decide when to spend on civil resilience versus offensive suppression
mechanicIdeas: long-duration barrage waves with limited recovery windows
city resilience stat that affects economy and morale losses
hybrid level structure combining interception and cross-border pressure
